title: Promoting strength-ductility synergy by heterogeneous grain structure in a zirconium-alloyed Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel
abstract_summary: This study presents a single-phase heterogeneous grain structure in Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel achieved by adjusting Zr content and applying thermo-mechanical treatment. With 0.50 wt % Zr, a bimodal grain structure composed of soft coarse grains (~5 μm, 65 % area) and hard fine grains (~1 μm, 35 % area) is formed due to the retarding effect of recrystallization from Zr solute drag and ZrC Zener pinning. The synergistic effects of microstructural heterogeneity and ZrC precipitation enhance strength, while multistage deformation mechanisms including heterogeneous deformation-induced hardening, dislocation slip, and nanotwinning provide significant strain hardening capability and ductility.
research_objective: To design a single-phase heterogeneous grain structure in Fe-Mn-Al-C low-density steel that mitigates strain localization and hetero-interfacial cracking, thereby achieving a superior strength-ductility synergy.
main_process_variables: Vacuum arc melting with high-purity raw materials (>99.8 wt %), high vacuum (<1e-6 bar), and high-purity argon protection; repeated melting at least five times
Hot rolling with 40 % thickness reduction, water quenching
Annealing at 900 °C for 5 min, followed by water quenching
Room-temperature tensile testing at strain rate 1e-3 s⁻¹
Quasi-in-situ tensile testing with EBSD and micro-DIC at global strains 0 %, 2 %, 8 %
